title: Community translations of the courses (per-language)
context: Translation of patient-facing cancer statistics courses is treated as HIGH riskTier because a mistranslated statistic or a subtly altered framing in patient-facing content reintroduces clinical risk — potentially the very misconception the course was designed to cure, now with the authority of a translated 'educational resource'. The translation-readiness extraction (oncology-data-literacy-a11y-018) structures strings so statistical claims remain keyed to citations, making per-language expert review tractable. Each language requires its own expert review of the translation by a qualified bilingual reviewer in the relevant domain. Only languages for which such a reviewer can be secured should proceed.
objective: Produce community translations of the Oncology-Data-Literacy courses for one or more additional languages, with each translation reviewed by a qualified bilingual expert (biostatistician/epidemiologist for statistical content; oncologist + patient-advocate for patient-facing survival and screening content) before publication. Translations must preserve all statistical claims exactly, maintain the 'Education only — not medical advice' framing in the target language, and keep every claim keyed to its register citation.
acceptanceCriteria: Each translated course has undergone per-language expert review by a qualified bilingual reviewer appropriate to the content (biostatistician/epidemiologist for statistical modules; oncologist + patient-advocate for patient-facing survival and screening modules)
Oncologist sign-off and patient-advocate sign-off recorded per language for all HIGH-tier (survival and screening) translated content before publication
All translated statistical claims are verified as accurate equivalents of the source claims; no statistical values, units, or qualifiers are altered or omitted in translation
'Education only — not medical advice' framing is present and accurately rendered in the target language on every translated module
All statistical claims in the translation remain keyed to their register citation entries (provenance is preserved through translation)
Translation is submitted as a PR following the expert-gated contribution process from oncology-data-literacy-ops-021
output: Expert-reviewed translation(s) of the Oncology-Data-Literacy courses in one or more additional languages, with all statistical claims preserved, HIGH-tier framing upheld, and per-language oncologist + patient-advocate sign-off recorded for patient-facing content.
